data.title: Lyell Immunopharma registers 625,000 shares for resale
data.description:
• Lyell Immunopharma (NASDAQ:LYEL) registers 625,000 common shares for resale by selling stockholders.
  • Shares were issued as additional merger consideration under the October 2024 merger agreement with ImmPACT Bio.
  • Issuance triggered by achievement of a clinical milestone tied to ImmPACT Bio’s next-gen CAR T-cell product.
  • Milestone was met, and shares issued to stockholders on July 2, 2025.
  • Lyell will not receive any proceeds from the resale.

data.title: Western Alliance raises 2025 net interest income and noninterest income outlook to 8–10% growth as margin rebounds
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Western Alliance Bancorporation (WAL) Q2 2025

Management View

  • Kenneth A. Vecchione, CEO, announced a major CFO succession, stating that "After the new year, Dale will transition his CFO responsibilities to Vishal following a thorough transition period. In his new role as Chief Banking Officer of Deposit Initiatives and Innovation, Dale will oversee 6 stand-alone deposit verticals, which generate strong liquidity." He emphasized that "Western Alliance again delivered strong financial results, exceeding expectations in the second quarter as strong business momentum drove a meaningful acceleration across a broad array of financial metrics."
  • The company generated over $1 billion of sequential loan growth for the second straight quarter and nearly $2 billion of quarterly deposit growth, with net interest margin rising 6 basis points sequentially to above 3.5%. Vecchione noted, "Return on average tangible common equity of 14.9% and return on average assets of 1.1% were both notably higher from Q1."
  • The bank will unify 6 legacy division bank brands under the Western Bank brand by year-end, reflecting its evolution into a larger national bank.
  • Dale M. Gibbons, CFO, stated, "Net interest income of almost $700 million, grew 7.2% quarter-over-quarter or nearly 29% annualized, driving PPNR up of $331 million for the quarter which equates to a 19% increase from Q1."

Outlook

  • Vecchione reiterated full-year loan and deposit growth targets of $5 billion and $8 billion, respectively. The net interest income outlook was revised higher to "8% to 10% growth" for the year due to delayed rate cuts and strong variable rate loan growth.
  • The net interest margin for the full year is expected to approximate 2024's upper 3.5% level. Noninterest income outlook was also revised upward to "8% to 10% growth."
  • Noninterest expense is now projected at 1% to 4% growth for the year, with ECR-related deposit costs forecast between $550 million and $590 million for the year and $170 million to $180 million in Q3.
  • Asset quality guidance remains at full-year net charge-offs of approximately 20 basis points, and the effective tax rate forecast is unchanged at approximately 20%.

Financial Results

  • Net interest income reached $698 million, a $47 million increase from Q1, driven by strong loan growth and a $3.3 billion rise in average earning assets.
  • Noninterest income rose 16.4% quarter-over-quarter to $148 million. Mortgage loan production volume increased 25% from last year, with mortgage banking revenue at approximately $78 million.
  • The adjusted efficiency ratio improved from 56% in Q1 to 52%.
  • Provision expense was $40 million due to organic loan growth and a replenishment of approximately $30 million in net charge-offs. The allowance for credit losses for funded loans moved $6 million higher from the prior quarter to $395 million.
  • HFI loans grew $1.2 billion quarterly, with C&I accounting for over two-thirds of the growth. Deposits grew $1.8 billion in Q2, including $400 million of growth from the digital asset banking program.
  • Tangible book value per share increased $1.77 from Q1 to $55.87.

Q&A

  • Christopher Edward McGratty, KBW: Asked about the CFO transition and deposit growth cadence. Gibbons said, "I'm just delighted to be stepping into that," while Vecchione assured, "this transition does not signal a change in the direction for the bank."
  • Jared David Wesley Shaw, Barclays: Queried fee income drivers and expense guidance. Vecchione indicated fee growth will come from "an increase in generally mortgage-related revenue from seasonal activities and also commercial banking activities," and Gibbons said, "The mortgage industry had a muted spring selling season... I think some of it as things stabilize might come forward."
  • Matthew Timothy Clark, Piper Sandler: Inquired about margin and fee income. Gibbons stated, "our margin is going to continue to inflect positively from here, both on a reported basis as well as an adjusted basis."
  • Timur Felixovich Braziler, Wells Fargo: Asked about funding strategy and OREO properties. Vecchione explained, "We took in 5 properties... the revenues that the properties generate are in excess of the expenses." Gibbons and Bruckner affirmed the adequacy of the allowance for credit losses.
  • David Charles Smith, Truist Securities: Probed the deposit cost outlook. Gibbons noted, "We're also showing that we do have a rate decrease in September and another one in December."
  • Ebrahim Huseini Poonawala, Bank of America: Asked about deposit strategies and regulatory changes. Gibbons saw "an opportunity to execute on these changes" but cautioned against crossing the $100 billion LFI threshold prematurely.
  • Casey Haire, Autonomous Research: Queried credit quality confidence and loan portfolio trends. Gibbons said, "This is the first time that we've called that we believe we're at a top" for criticized assets, and Bruckner confirmed confidence in portfolio strategy.
  • Robert Andrew Terrell, Stephens: Asked about OREO property trends. Bruckner responded, "We expect to see this flat to declining as we move forward."
  • Bernard Von Gizycki, Deutsche Bank: Inquired about insurance costs and equity investments. Gibbons said, "It's largely behind us," regarding insurance cost migration, and expects steady performance on equity investments.
  • Anthony Albert Elian, JPMorgan: Asked about ECR deposit cost sensitivity and digital asset deposit growth. Gibbons indicated a current limit of 4% for digital asset deposits but sees "room to have that continue to move forward."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts were generally constructive but raised pointed questions about deposit growth cadence, fee income sustainability, expense control, and asset quality, reflecting a neutral to slightly positive tone.
  • Management displayed a confident tone during prepared remarks, with Vecchione emphasizing, "We continue to target upper teens return on tangible common equity as our near-term profitability North Star," and Gibbons expressing enthusiasm about his new role and business line trajectories.
  • During Q&A, management maintained confidence, with Gibbons stating, "we are absolutely comfortable with the values that we're carrying in our ACL," and Vecchione reiterating focus on organic growth and prudent capital management.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone was more assertive about reaching a peak in criticized assets and more optimistic on revised revenue outlooks. Analyst tone remained steady, with questions focusing on execution and risk management.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The Q2 call featured a major management transition with the CFO succession plan, compared to Q1's continuity.
  • Guidance on net interest income and noninterest income was revised higher to 8% to 10% growth, up from the prior 6% to 8% outlook.
  • Net interest margin rebounded above 3.5% in Q2, while tangible book value per share continued to increase.
  • Management displayed increased confidence in reaching a peak in criticized assets, while analysts maintained focus on deposit growth, fee income, and asset quality.
  • The Q2 call included more discussion of digital asset banking growth and its contribution to deposit expansion.
  • Strategic focus shifted toward unifying bank brands and expanding technology and innovation banking.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management highlighted potential regulatory impacts on growth, emphasizing cautious deposit strategies to avoid prematurely crossing the $100 billion LFI threshold.
  • Analysts questioned the sustainability of deposit growth, fee income sources, and expense management amid ongoing investment in technology and regulatory readiness.
  • The increase in OREO properties was addressed, with management confident that these assets "will not impact charge-off behavior, unless there is a significant decline in the revenues or the valuations in those markets."
  • Management reiterated their confidence in the adequacy of the allowance for credit losses and capital levels, even as nonperforming assets rose.

Final Takeaway

Western Alliance delivered another strong quarter, highlighted by upward revisions to both net interest income and noninterest income guidance for 2025 and a reaffirmed commitment to organic growth. The CFO succession marks a notable leadership transition, while strategic initiatives such as brand unification and expansion in technology and digital asset banking continue to drive momentum. Management anticipates sustained margin strength and asset quality stability, with a focus on prudent deposit growth and capital management as the company approaches key regulatory thresholds.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: David Ellison met with FCC's Carr to discuss Paramount-Skydance deal and obtain approval
data.description:
Top boss of Skydance Media David Ellison met with Federal Communications Commission Chair Brendan Carr this week to request regulatory approval for the pending Paramount Global (NASDAQ:PARA) (NASDAQ:PARAA) transaction, which would create a $28B enterprise.

At the meeting, which was also attended by Carr's office members Greg Watson and Ben Arden, Ellison highlighted the public interest benefits of Skydance buying Paramount, according to a letter filed with the FCC.

Ellison said Paramount would thrive under his new leadership, and his strategy would drive long-term growth for the company amid the challenging media landscape in the U.S.

He assured the government agency that the transaction would make sure CBS network's editorial decisions reflect "the varied ideological perspectives of American viewers" and "unbiased journalism." 

The Paramount-Skydance deal hit a roadblock after President Donald Trump sued the network over a "60 Minutes" interview on CBS with then Vice President Kamala Harris.

The lawsuit, which was recently settled for $16M, had triggered the exit of the show's executive producer, Bill Owens, and CBS CEO Wendy McMahon.

Additionally, Ellison clarified that the new entity would not have any Chinese influence via Tencent (OTCPK:TCEHY), a company classified as a "Chinese Military Company" by the U.S.

Tencent's minority stake in Ellison's company had raised national security concerns for the Paramount-Skydance deal.

He reiterated that Tencent will hold only a non-voting, passive interest of less than 5% and will have no ability to influence the operation of the newly formed company.

data.title: U.S. drilling rigs turn higher after 11 straight weekly declines, Baker Hughes says
data.description:
The number of total oil and gas drilling rigs in the U.S. gained 7 to 544, rising for the first time in 12 weeks and posting the biggest weekly increase this year, Baker Hughes reported Friday in its latest survey.

The number of rigs drilling for oil in the U.S. dropped 2 to 422 this week, extending its decline to the lowest count since September 2021, while gas rigs gained 9 to 117, and 5 rigs remained classified as miscellaneous.

Despite this week's overall rig increase, Baker Hughes said the total count was still down 42 rigs, or 7%, below this time last year.

In Texas, the biggest oil and gas producing state, the total rig count fell by 2 to 253, the lowest since October 2021; in the Permian Basin, located in western Texas and eastern New Mexico, the rig count also declined by 2 to 263, also the lowest since October 2021.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

data.title: Virax Biolabs receives Nasdaq letter over non-compliance with minimum bid price rule
data.description:
• Virax Biolabs Group (NASDAQ:VRAX) received a notice from Nasdaq notifying the company that it is not in compliance with its minimum bid price requirement.
  • The company has a compliance period of one hundred eighty (180) calendar days, or until January 12, 2026 to regain compliance with Nasdaq's minimum bid price requirement
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: HOOKIPA Pharma to delist from Nasdaq and liquidate following Gilead asset sale
data.description:
• HOOKIPA Pharma (NASDAQ:HOOK) announced voluntary delisting from the Nasdaq Capital Market.
  • The company also intends to deregister its common stock and suspend SEC reporting obligations.
  • Delisting follows an asset sale agreement with Gilead Sciences (GILD), transferring key assets from HB-400 (hepatitis B) and parts of HB-500 (HIV) programs.
  • A special stockholder meeting is scheduled for July 29, 2025, to vote on the asset sale and proposed company dissolution. Upon approval, HOOKIPA will file for dissolution in Delaware and begin liquidation proceedings.
  • Form 25 for delisting expected to be filed July 29, becoming effective around August 8, 2025.
  • The decision is aimed at reducing costs and maximizing distributions to shareholders during the wind-down.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: CrowdStrike ends in green, trimming losses after six-session slide
data.description:
CrowdStrike Holdings (NASDAQ:CRWD) snapped six consecutive sessions of losses on Friday as it inched up 1.30% to close at $475.94.

The stock entered the red territory on July 10 after CFRA downgraded the cybersecurity stalwart from Buy to Hold over its high valuation. However, the brokerage increased its price target on the stock to $555 from $517.

“We note that valuation is at a three-year high (forward EV/S of 24.4x) from strong recent price momentum (+58% since April lows), and we see limited upside in the near term as expectations for 2H FY 26 ARR acceleration are priced in at this point, in our view," CFRA analyst Janice Quek said.

Since then, the stock has lost nearly 4%. But on a year-to-date basis, CRWD has gained 37.37% outpacing the 7.10% rise in the broader markets.

The stock’s downward trajectory in the week was further fuelled after Morgan Stanley also downgraded the stock from Overweight to Equal-weight.

Analysts led by Keith Weiss said they were "stepping to the sidelines given full valuation after ~50% run and rising growth expectations." They still view Crowdstrike as a strong long-term story, but noted that the anticipated second-half acceleration now appears to be largely priced into the stock.

Seeking Alpha analyst Hunting Alpha also downgraded the stock from Buy to Hold due to a stretched valuation, which the analyst believes leaves little margin of safety at current levels.

“CRWD stock is trading at a 1-year fwd PE of 127.4x, a 135% premium to the 54.2x median of its comps. This 135% premium is much higher than the typical 64% premium that CRWD has commanded over other cybersecurity companies. Hence, I think there is a limited margin of safety in CRWD stock right now,” they said.

Overall, Seeking Alpha analysts have rated CRWD as Hold, while Wall Street analysts have a consensus rating of Buy. As per Seeking Alpha’s quant rating, CRWD has been rated a Strong Buy with a score of 4.78 out of 5. It has been graded A for growth and profitability, but has a D+ for valuation.

data.title: MetroCity Bankshares GAAP EPS of $0.65 beats by $0.01, revenue of $37.91M beats by $1.31M

data.title: Choice Properties Real Estate Investment Trust reports Q2 results
data.description:
• Choice Properties Real Estate Investment Trust press release (OTC:PPRQF): Q2 net loss of $154.2 million compared to net income of $513.2 million in the same prior year period.

data.title: Matson shares slide as Jefferies downgrades on demand uncertainty
data.description:
Matson (NYSE:MATX) -6.2% in Friday's trading to its lowest in more than two months as Jefferies downgraded the marine shipping company to Hold from Buy with a $115 price target, cut from $135, expecting downside risks to freight rates from the extraordinary figures seen in 2024.

Jefferies analyst Omar Nokta said he expects Matson (NYSE:MATX) will continue to benefit from its niche Jones Act platform that provides a solid earnings profile while having optionality on international trades, and Red Sea diversions likely will persist for longer given recent attacks, which should keep global market supply tighter and freight rates supported at higher levels, but "given uncertainty on demand especially given a weak peak season thus far gives us pause."

Freight rates have been volatile, Nokta noted, with the swings seen over the past three months
generally not seen historically; spot rates began April at $2,500/feu, surged to $6,000/feu by early June, and have since dropped again closer to $2,000/feu on softer bookings and returning container capacity.

The analyst modeled a 15% Y/Y decline in Matson's (MATX) Q3 China volumes and a 12% drop for 2025 overall, after slipping 1.4% in Q1, and lowered his Q3 earnings estimate to $2.38/share from $3.49/share and full-year EPS to $8.99 from $10.64.
